contentBlocks.original:
चाम्पेयगौरार्ध शरीरकायै कर्पूरगौरार्ध शरीरकाय ।
धम्मिल्लकायै च जटाधराय नमः शिवयै च नमः शिवाय ॥1॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
cāmpeyagaurārdha śarīrakāyai karpūragaurārdha śarīrakāya .
dhammillakāyai ca jaṭādharāya namaḥ śivayai ca namaḥ śivāya ..1..
contentBlocks.translation:
I bow to the form
Where half of the form of Goddess Parvati was with the complexion of the Champaka flowers and
the other half of God Shiva is present with a complexion like that of camphor
The form, where the half of the head (of Parvati) is braided beautifully with a chignon, while the other half (of God Shiva) is with the matted hair
I bow to you, Goddess Parvathi and God Shiva in the form of Ardhanareeswara
contentBlocks.meanings: चाम्पेयगौरार्ध (cāmpeyagaurārdha) – half with Champaka complexion (cāmpeya:Champaka flower | gaura:fair complexion | ardha:half); शरीरकायै (śarīrakāyai) – to the body; कर्पूरगौरार्ध (karpūragaurārdha) – half with camphor complexion (karpūra:camphor | gaura:fair complexion | ardha:half); शरीरकाय (śarīrakāya) – to the body; धम्मिल्लकायै (dhammillakāyai) – to the braided hair; च (ca) – and; जटाधराय (jaṭādharāya) – to the matted hair (jaṭā:matted hair | dharāya:to the bearer); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवयै (śivayai) – to Goddess Parvati; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवाय (śivāya) – to God Shiva;

contentBlocks.original:
कस्तूरिका कुङ्कुमचर्चितायै चितारजःपुञ्जविचर्चिताय ।
कृतस्मरायै विकृतस्मराय नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय ||2||
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kastūrikā kuṅkumacarcitāyai citārajaḥpuñja vicarcitāya .
kṛtasmarāyai vikṛtasmarāya namaḥ śivāyai ca namaḥ śivāya ..2..
contentBlocks.translation:
I bow to the form
The side of Goddess Parvathi is smeared with Kasturi’s scents (Kasturi musk) and other Kumkumas.
While the half of God Shiva is smeared with the Chitha Bhasma (ashes from the funeral pyre)
The form that’s with splendid looks (with adorned jewels and clothes) on the side of Parvati and with the dreadful looks (due to elephant skin, tiger skin, and serpents) on the side of Shiva
I bow to you, Goddess Parvathi and God Shiva in the form of Ardhanareeswara
contentBlocks.meanings: कस्तूरिका (kastūrikā) – musk; कुङ्कुमचर्चितायै (kuṅkumacarcitāyai) – smeared with kumkuma (kuṅkuma:kumkuma | carcitāyai:smeared with); चितारजःपुञ्जविचर्चिताय (citārajaḥpuñjavicarcitāya) – smeared with funeral ashes (citā:funeral pyre | rajaḥ:ashes | puñja:heap | vicarcitāya:smeared with); कृतस्मरायै (kṛtasmarāyai) – with splendid looks (kṛta:made | smarāyai:looks); विकृतस्मराय (vikṛtasmarāya) – with dreadful looks (vikṛta:changed | smarāya:looks); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवायै (śivāyai) – to Goddess Parvati; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवाय (śivāya) – to God Shiva;

contentBlocks.original:
झणत्क्वणत्कङ्कणनूपुरायै पादाब्जराजत्फणिनूपुराय ।
हेमाङ्गदायै भुजगाङ्गदाय नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय ||3||
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jhaṇatkvaṇatkaṅ kaṇanūpurāyai pādābjarājat phaṇinūpurāya .
hemāṅgadāyai bhujagāṅgadāya namaḥ śivāyai ca namaḥ śivāya ..3..
contentBlocks.translation:
I bow to the form
In which the half of Goddess Parvati is adorned with the pleasant sound making ornaments worn on the wrists (Kankanas) and those of ankles (Nupuras)
While in the other half, the lotus feet of God Shiva adorned with the serpents as the anklets
The excellent form, where the half of Parvati is with the ornaments of Gold, while the other half (of Shiva) is adorned with serpents as ornaments
I bow to you, Goddess Parvathi and God Shiva in the form of Ardhanareeswara
contentBlocks.meanings: झणत्क्वणत्कङ्कणनूपुरायै (jhaṇatkvaṇatkaṅkaṇanūpurāyai) – to her with jingling anklets (jhaṇat:jingling | kvaṇat:sounding | kaṅkaṇa:bracelet | nūpura:anklet | āyai:to her); पादाब्जराजत्फणिनूपुराय (pādābjarājatphaṇinūpurāya) – to him with serpent anklets (pāda:foot | abja:lotus | rājat:shining | phaṇi:serpent | nūpura:anklet | āya:to him); हेमाङ्गदायै (hemāṅgadāyai) – to her with golden armlets (hema:gold | aṅgada:armlet | āyai:to her); भुजगाङ्गदाय (bhujagāṅgadāya) – to him with serpent armlets (bhujaga:serpent | aṅgada:armlet | āya:to him); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवायै (śivāyai) – to Goddess Parvati; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवाय (śivāya) – to God Shiva;

contentBlocks.original:
विशालनीलोत्पललोचनायै विकासिपङ्केरु हलोचनाय ।
समेक्षणायै विषमेक्षणाय नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय ॥4॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśālanīlot palalocanāyai vikāsipaṅkeru halocanāya .
samekṣaṇāyai viṣamekṣaṇāya namaḥ śivāyai ca namaḥ śivāya ..4..
contentBlocks.translation:
I bow to the form
Where the eyes of Goddess Parvathi are with a great look, long, extended, and looking like the blue lotuses (neelothphala flower) and
The eyes of God Shiva are looking bright like the blossomed red lotuses
The composite form of the two-eyed Goddess and the three-eyed God
I bow to you, Goddess Parvathi and God Shiva in the form of Ardhanareeswara
contentBlocks.meanings: विशाल (viśāla) – great; नीलोत्पल (nīlotpala) – blue lotus (nīla:blue | utpala:lotus); लोचनायै (locanāyai) – to the eyes; विकासि (vikāsi) – blossomed; पङ्केरुह (paṅkeruha) – red lotus (paṅka:mud | ruha:growing); लोचनाय (locanāya) – to the eyes; समेक्षणायै (samekṣaṇāyai) – to the two-eyed (sama:equal | īkṣaṇāyai:to the eyes); विषमेक्षणाय (viṣamekṣaṇāya) – to the three-eyed (viṣama:unequal | īkṣaṇāya:to the eyes); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवायै (śivāyai) – to Goddess Parvathi; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवाय (śivāya) – to God Shiva;

contentBlocks.original:
मन्दारमाला कलितालकायै कपालमालाङ्कितकन्धराय ।
दिव्याम्बरायै च दिगम्बराय नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय ||5||
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mandāramālā kalitālakāyai kapālamālāṅkita kandharāya .
divyāmbarāyai ca digambarāya namaḥ śivāyai ca namaḥ śivāya ..5..
contentBlocks.translation:
I bow to the form
In which the hair of Goddess Parvati is decorated with the garland of Hibiscus flowers and
the neck of the God Shiva is adorned with the garland of skulls
O the extraordinary form in which the goddess side is with the great garments, while the other half of God Shiva is without any clothing
I bow to you, Goddess Parvathi and God Shiva in the form of Ardhanareeswara
contentBlocks.meanings: मन्दारमाला (mandāramālā) – garland of Hibiscus flowers (mandāra:Hibiscus | mālā:garland); कलितालकायै (kalitālakāyai) – to the one with decorated hair (kalita:decorated | alakāyai:to the hair); कपालमालाङ्कितकन्धराय (kapālamālāṅkitakandharāya) – to the one with skull garlanded neck (kapāla:skull | mālā:garland | aṅkita:marked | kandharāya:to the neck); दिव्याम्बरायै (divyāmbarāyai) – to the one with divine garments (divya:divine | ambarāyai:to the garments); च (ca) – and; दिगम्बराय (digambarāya) – to the one without clothing (dig:direction | ambarāya:to the clothing); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवायै (śivāyai) – to Goddess Parvati; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवाय (śivāya) – to God Shiva;

contentBlocks.original:
अम्भोधरश्यामलकुन्तलायै तडित्प्रभाताम्रजटाधराय ।
निरीश्वरायै निखिलेश्वराय नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय ||6||
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ambhodharaśyāmal̤akuntal̤āyai taḍitprabhātāmra jaṭādharāya .
nirīśvarāyai nikhileśvarāya namaḥ śivāyai ca namaḥ śivāya ..6..
contentBlocks.translation:
I bow to the form
Where the hair of Goddess Parvathi is with the black colour like that of water-bearing dark clouds
While the hair locks of God Shiva are looking shining in the colour of copper
O the one who doesn’t have a superior being over him, who is the great God and the God of the entire cosmos
I bow to you, Goddess Parvathi and God Shiva in the form of Ardhanareeswara
contentBlocks.meanings: अम्भोधरश्यामलकुन्तलायै (ambhodharaśyāmalakuntalāyai) – to the one with dark hair (ambhodhara:water-bearing cloud | śyāmala:dark | kuntalāyai:to the hair); तडित्प्रभाताम्रजटाधराय (taḍitprabhātāmrajaṭādharāya) – to the one with coppery locks (taḍit:lightning | prabhā:shining | tāmra:copper | jaṭā:locks | dharāya:to the bearer); निरीश्वरायै (nirīśvarāyai) – to the one without superior (nir:without | īśvarāyai:to the superior); निखिलेश्वराय (nikhileśvarāya) – to the lord of all (nikhila:all | īśvarāya:to the lord); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवायै (śivāyai) – to Goddess Parvathi; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवाय (śivāya) – to God Shiva;

contentBlocks.original:
प्रपञ्चसृष्ट्युन्मुखलास्यकायै समस्तसंहारक ताण्डवाय ।
जगज्जनन्यै जगदेकपित्रे नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय ||7||
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prapañcasṛṣṭyun mukhalāsyakāyai samastasaṃhāraka tāṇḍavāya .
jagajjananyai jagadekapitre namaḥ śivāyai ca namaḥ śivāya ..7..
contentBlocks.translation:
I bow to the form
By which the creation of the cosmos begins with the gentle smile on the face of Goddess Parvati and
By which the entire cosmos will get reunited back to the Shivaa by the tandava dance
O the Mother and the Father of the Cosmos
I bow to you, Goddess Parvathi and God Shiva in the form of Ardhanareeswara
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रपञ्चसृष्ट्युन्मुखलास्यकायै (prapañcasṛṣṭyunmukhalāsyakāyai) – to the form of cosmic creation (prapañca:cosmos | sṛṣṭi:creation | unmukha:beginning | lāsya:dance | kāyai:to the form); समस्तसंहारक (samastasaṃhāraka) – destroyer of all (samasta:all | saṃhāraka:destroyer); ताण्डवाय (tāṇḍavāya) – to the dance; जगज्जनन्यै (jagajjananyai) – to the mother of the world (jagat:world | jananyai:to the mother); जगदेकपित्रे (jagadekapitre) – to the sole father of the world (jagat:world | eka:sole | pitre:to the father); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवायै (śivāyai) – to Goddess Parvati; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवाय (śivāya) – to God Shiva;

contentBlocks.original:
प्रदीप्तरत्नोज्ज्वल कुण्डलायै स्फुरन्महापन्नगभूषणाय ।
शिवान्वितायै च शिवान्विताय नमः शिवायै च नमः शिवाय ||8॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pradīptaratnojjvala kuṇḍalāyai sphuranmahāpannaga bhūṣaṇāya .
śivānvitāyai ca śivānvitāya namaḥ śivāyai ca namaḥ śivāya ..8..
contentBlocks.translation:
I bow to the form
Of which the Parvathi’s form is looking resplendent with the precious gem studded earrings
And the form of God Shiva is looking great wearing the great serpents as his ornaments
O the one who united with Shiva (Goddess Parvathi) and the one who united with Shivaa (God Shiva)
I bow to you, Goddess Parvathi and God Shiva in the form of Ardhanareeswara
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रदीप्तरत्नोज्ज्वल (pradīptaratnojjvala) – shining with bright jewels (pradīpta:shining | ratna:jewel | ujjvala:bright); कुण्डलायै (kuṇḍalāyai) – to the one with earrings; स्फुरन्महापन्नगभूषणाय (sphuranmahāpannagabhūṣaṇāya) – adorned with great serpents (sphuran:shining | mahā:great | pannaga:serpent | bhūṣaṇa:ornament); शिवान्वितायै (śivānvitāyai) – united with Shiva (śiva:Shiva | anvitāyai:united with); च (ca) – and; शिवान्विताय (śivānvitāya) – united with Shivaa (śiva:Shivaa | anvitāya:united with); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवायै (śivāyai) – to Goddess Parvathi; च (ca) – and; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; शिवाय (śivāya) – to God Shiva;

contentBlocks.original:
फलश्रुतिः
एतत्पठेदष्टकमिष्टदं यो भक्त्या स मान्यो भुवि दीर्घजीवी ।
प्राप्नोति सौभाग्यमनन्तकालं भूयात्सदा तस्य समस्तसिद्धिः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phalaśrutiḥ
etatpaṭhedaṣṭakamiṣṭadaṃ yo bhaktyā sa mānyo bhuvi dīrghajīvī .
prāpnoti saubhāgyamanantakālaṃ bhūyātsadā tasya samastasiddhiḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation:
One who recites this Ashtakam with great interest or devotion
They will become great beings, receive great honor, and long life in the bhuloka
And for those will be granted good fortune and auspiciousness the way long
And will also be granted all the siddhi’s.
contentBlocks.meanings: फलश्रुतिः (phalaśrutiḥ) – result of recitation (phala:result | śrutiḥ:recitation); एतत् (etat) – this; पठेत् (paṭhet) – should recite; अष्टकम् (aṣṭakam) – eight verses; इष्टदम् (iṣṭadam) – granting desires (iṣṭa:desired | dam:granting); यः (yaḥ) – who; भक्त्या (bhaktyā) – with devotion; स (sa) – he; मान्यः (mānyaḥ) – honored; भुवि (bhuvi) – on earth; दीर्घजीवी (dīrghajīvī) – long-lived (dīrgha:long | jīvī:lived); प्राप्नोति (prāpnoti) – obtains; सौभाग्यम् (saubhāgyam) – good fortune; अनन्तकालम् (anantakālam) – eternal time (ananta:eternal | kālam:time); भूयात् (bhūyāt) – may it be; सदा (sadā) – always; तस्य (tasya) – his; समस्तसिद्धिः (samastasiddhiḥ) – all accomplishments (samasta:all | siddhiḥ:accomplishments);
